title: dryopteris affinis var. disjuncta
intro: Dryopteris affinis var. disjuncta, also known as the goldenback fern, is a variety of the autumn fern, a type of deciduous fern native to Asia and Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, as well as reduce swelling and pain. The health benefits of the goldenback fern include its ability to support respiratory health, reduce fever, and act as a natural diuretic. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and uses.
